feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP B1 Crystal Report
entry.content: Can anyone help me design in crystal report for purchase quotation comparison like screenshot attached.I am using SAP B1 v10 HANA version. When I place vendors who quoted against purchase quotation in detail section, it displays vendors vertically in one row but my client mandates me to design in such a way that when they enter doc num for the purchase request, it should show all the vendors  horizontally and items(from purchase request) vertically. they want exactly same report as screenshot.Additonally there should sub columns like price ,total,BST under each vendor.I thought cross tab would work but I am not able to achieve it.I would be grateful if anyone help me on it

entry.title: Check the authorization of the user using SAP B1 SDK
entry.content:
I am using SAP B1 10.0  (10.00.260) SP 2408. In sdk, I want to check the authorization of goods issue and inventory transfer. I have used this code .  While executing the function GetPermission , I am getting this error,

Public member 'UserPermissions' on type 'IApplication' not found.

  

If GetPermission("3072") = "boNo" Then
    SBO_Application.MessageBox("You don't have permission for Stock Transfer.Please contact IT Department.")
    BubbleEvent = False
    Exit Sub
End If
If GetPermission("10001") = "boNo" Then
    SBO_Application.MessageBox("You don't have permission for Goods Issue.Please contact IT Department.")
    BubbleEvent = False
    Exit Sub
End If

 Public Function GetPermission(ByRef strForm As String)
     Dim strPermission As BoYesNoEnum

     Try
         strPermission = SBO_Application.UserPermissions.GetSystemPermission(strForm)
         Return strPermission
     Catch ex As Exception

     End Try
 End Function

entry.title: While Adding A/P Invoice from Draft Reconciliation Difference Error
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are facing an issue in the following scenario:

An A/P Invoice was created based on a GRPO.
There is an existing A/P Down Payment Request that is linked to this invoice.
The A/P Invoice was saved in Draft status.
Now, when the user attempts to add the invoice from the Document Draft Report, the system displays the following error message:

"Reconciliation difference must be zero before reconciling [Message 3821-8]"

Need help in troubleshooting this issue.

entry.title: service layer error -1116
entry.content:
We recently upgraded to SAP Business One 10.0.

I'm currently testing some Service Layer (SL) features to integrate with our .NET ASP core application.

The login test via Postman works perfectly. However, when attempting to cancel a sales order using the following endpoint:

 

POST /b1s/v1/Orders(xxxx)/Cancel

I receive this error response:

{ "error": { "code": -1116, "message": { "lang": "en-us", "value": "Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction" } } }

In the SAP client/server, we couldn't find the folder or location where Service Layer logs are stored to get more detailed information.

Has anyone faced this issue or knows how to resolve it?
Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Modelo de autorización de SAP para SAP BUSINESS ONE SALES
entry.content:
Buenos días expertos consultas

¿es posible activar procedimientos de autorización desde la app Business One Sales con consultas de usuario?

¿o aplica esta nota?

1681542 - Error al desencadenar procedimientos de autorización por consultas de usuario

 

@fe

entry.title: SAPBusinessOne-How to use Formatted Search to Copy the value from header to row for Whse in SO?
entry.content:
SAP Business One - How to use Formatted Search to Copy the value of Customer Code (CardCode) from header to row value for Warehouse in Sales Order?

Note: The Business Partner Code (CardCode) and Warehouse Code are manually inputted with the same value. e.g. Business Partner Code = BP1001 and Warehouse code = BP1001. This setup meant that each Customer has an assigned Warehouse with the same value.

entry.title: Discount % in Goods Issue
entry.content:
Hi community,

There is a confusing for which I am writing this , In goods issue screen the total was not matching with the calculation, info price * qty but when enabled the Discount % column from form settings, here we can see the discount percentage has been calculated without manual intervention for which the total are not matching as per formula.

Could anyone let me know how this discount % is calculated automatically during goods issue , in this case the inventory valuation method is FIFO.

entry.title: Inventory Audit Report Shows Negative Cumulative Stock Value (SAP B1 10.0 FP2208)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I encountered an issue where the Inventory Audit Report for a specific item shows a negative Cumulative Value.

Below are the details of the scenario:

The item uses "Moving Average" valuation and currently has 0 stock.

1). When running the Inventory Audit Report for "All Warehouses", the cumulative stock value is 0.00.

2). However, when running the report for each warehouse individually:

Warehouse A shows a cumulative stock value of -10,000.

Warehouse B shows -5.

Warehouse C shows 10,005.

The combined total of these values is 0, which matches the "All Warehouses" result.

Is this behavior considered normal?

Additional notes:

The system setting “Manage Item Cost per Warehouse” is not enabled.

SAP B1 version is "SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.201) FP 2208 (64-bit)".

Localization: UK International / Republic of Ireland.

Troubleshooting steps already taken:

Executed Inventory Revaluation to 0 the moving average cost.

Executed Restore Item Balances.

Followed the solution mentioned in this SAP Community thread, but the issue persists warehouse-specific audit reports still show individual values.

 Inventory Audit Shows Negative Cumulative Stock Value

Question:
How can I completely clear or zero out this item's stock value across all three warehouses?

Best regards,
Rita

entry.title: Cost Center Not Appearing for Balance Sheet Account in Depreciation Journal Entry – SAP B1 HANA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have implemented Fixed Asset management in SAP Business One (HANA version) for a customer, and we’ve completed the configuration for depreciation posting. Everything is working fine, the depreciation run is generating journal entries as expected.

However, I’ve noticed that cost centers are only appearing for the P&L accounts (like Depreciation Expenses) in the journal entry. For the Balance Sheet side (like Accumulated Depreciation), the cost center field remains blank.

Here’s what I’ve verified:

  • In Chart of Accounts, the Balance Sheet account ("Accumulated Depreciation – Plant & Machinery") has ActType set to “Expenditure.”

  • The cost center is assigned at the asset master level.

  • Checked the box "Consolidate Journal Entry Rows by Distribution Rules" in Depreciation Run - Preview

I understand that SAP B1 only applies cost centers to P&L accounts, but since I’ve marked the BS account as “Expenditure” in ActType, I was expecting the cost center to reflect there as well.

Is there any way to force cost center assignment to Balance Sheet accounts in depreciation JE?
Or is this the standard behavior, regardless of the ActType setting?

I’ve attached a screenshot of the JE for reference (showing cost centers only on the expense lines).

 

Appreciate any insights or suggestions from those who’ve faced similar behavior.

Thanks in advance,
Sebastin

 

SAP Business One SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA

entry.title: Overview Page Fiori App for SAP Business One on SQL
entry.content: Is it possible to create overview page Fiori App for SAP Business one running on SQL server? SAP Business One

entry.title: Cannot update Picklist after updating source document
entry.content:
Hi, 

i ve got a problem and i need help. I am increasing picked quantity (over the released quantity) on picklist via service layer so I need to increase quantity on Sales order.

For Info:
Article has no prescribed batches on document before PATCH request and is managed using batch numbers and warehouse has NO BinLocations (Disabled BinLocations) 

I do PATCH /b1s/v2/Orders(1111)

{
  "DocEntry": 1413,
  "DocNum": 1111,
  "DocObjectCode": "oOrders",
  "DocumentsOwner": 5,
  "SalesPersonCode": 1,
  "DocDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "DocDueDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "TaxDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "CardCode": "Test",
  "CardName": "Test Test",
  "NumAtCard": "",
  "PaymentGroupCode": 4,
  "ReserveInvoice": "tNO",
  "AddressExtension": {
    "ShipToStreet": "Test",
    "ShipToBuilding": "",
    "ShipToCity": "Praha",
    "ShipToZipCode": "111 11",
    "ShipToCountry": "CZ",
    "BillToStreet": "Test",
    "BillToBuilding": "",
    "BillToCity": "Praha",
    "BillToZipCode": "111 11",
    "BillToCountry": "CZ",
    "DocEntry": 1413
  },
  "DocumentLines": [
    {
      "LineNum": 0,
      "VisualOrder": 0,
      "LineStatus": "bost_Open",
      "BaseType": -1,
      "ItemCode": "B3002",
      "ItemDescription": "Desc",
      "ShipDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
      "WarehouseCode": "01",
      "UseBaseUnits": "tNO",
      "RemainingOpenQuantity": 5.0,
      "InventoryQuantity": 5.2,
      "RemainingOpenInventoryQuantity": 5.0,
      "U_VAG_WHP_OriginalQtty": 4.0,
      "MeasureUnit": "",
      "UnitsOfMeasurment": 1.0,
      "UoMEntry": -1,
      "UoMCode": "Manuálně",
      "BatchNumbers": [
        {
          "BatchNumber": "AAA",
          "BaseLineNumber": 0,
          "Quantity": 5.2,
          "SystemSerialNumber": 2,
          "ItemCode": "TestItem"
        }
      ],
      "SerialNumbers": [],
      "DocumentLinesBinAllocations": [],
      "DocumentLineAdditionalExpenses": []
    }
  ],
  "DocumentAdditionalExpenses": [],
  "DocumentSpecialLines": []
}

PATCH request will Succeed and after that i try to update picklist 
PUT  /b1s/v2/PickLists(679)
{
    "Absoluteentry": 679,
    "Name": "Manager",
    "ObjectType": "156",
    "OwnerCode": 1,
    "OwnerName": null,
    "PickDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
    "Status": "ps_Released",
    "UseBaseUnits": "tNO",
    "PickListsLines": [
        {
            "AbsoluteEntry": 679,
            "LineNumber": 0,
            "OrderEntry": 1413,
            "OrderRowID": 0,
            "PickedQuantity": 5.2,
            "ReleasedQuantity": 0,
            "BaseObjectType": 17,
            "SerialNumbers": [],
            "BatchNumbers": [
                {
                    "BatchNumber": "AAA",
                    "BaseLineNumber": 0,
                    "SystemSerialNumber": 2,
                    "Quantity": 5.2,
                    "ItemCode": "TestItem"
                }
            ],
            "DocumentLinesBinAllocations": []
        }
    ]
}

Result =>
{
    "error": {
        "code": "-2039",
        "details": [
            {
                "code": "",
                "message": ""
            }
        ],
        "message": "Another user or another operation modified data; to continue, open the window again (ODBC -2039)"
    }
}

When i do the same with warehouse with bin locations and with aditional /b1s/v2/PickListsService_UpdateReleasedAllocation call, request will be OK, 
if i do the same with warehouse without bin locations and without batches (only PATCH Orders, PUT Picklists) request will be ok. 
What i should do different. I tried it also with PickListsService_UpdateReleasedAllocation call (the same ERROR), i tried PATCH instead of PUT (the same ERROR).
If you ve got any advice, any solution, i will be glad. 
Thanks.

entry.title: Intermittent Alert Delivery Issue in SAP B1 (Version 10.0 SP 2408)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have configured several alerts in SAP Business One (version 10.0 SP 2408 HANA), with both internal and email notifications enabled. The SMTP mail configuration is working correctly, and alerts are typically being sent without any issues.

However, I’ve observed an intermittent issue:
On certain days, neither the internal alerts nor the email alerts are triggered as expected. Interestingly, once the server is restarted the next day (our server operates from 08:00 AM to 08:00 PM), all the pending alerts from the previous day are sent out immediately after the server starts. This behavior occurs approximately twice a month.

Could anyone advise what might be causing this issue? Also, is there a recommended way to simulate or diagnose the problem?

Looking forward to your suggestions.

Best regards,
Sreesaj

entry.title: SLD 1000170: DB version and System DB Status not displayed for SAP Business One on MSSQL2017
entry.content:
Any known issues with blank Version/System DB Status in SLD 1000170?

After registering our SQL Server 2017 instance in the SLD “DB Instances” tab, the Version and System DB Status columns remain blank (“-------”). The only field populated is Database Authentication, and the Backup Path is empty. No companies appear in the “Companies” pane, and all schema‐related buttons (Export Schema, Import Schema, etc.) remain disabled.

 

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in to SLD as a user with Administrator privileges.

  2. Navigate to DB Instances and Companies → DB Instances.

  3. Click Add, enter

    • Server Name: Servername\TEST1

    • Server Type: MSSQL2017

    • Database User: <SQL user with sysadmin>

  4. Click Save.

  5. Result:

    • Version and System DB Status show “-------”

    • Backup Path remains blank

    • No companies can be imported or displayed

Expected Behavior

  • SLD should connect to the SQL instance, retrieve and display the database version and System DB Status (e.g. “Online”).

  • Registered SLD company schemas should appear, and schema‐management buttons (Export, Import, Deploy) become active

What We’ve Checked So Far

  • Verified network connectivity and SQL credentials.

  • Confirmed SQL user has appropriate system‐admin privileges.

  • Restarted the SLD server after driver deployment.

 

Component:

SAP Business One System Landscape Directory (SLD Version: 1000170)

Environment: Windows Server 2019; Database: Microsoft SQL Server 2017

entry.title: ERROR WHILE USING DTW
entry.content:
I am using SAP B1 10.0 Hana version.

I want to update minimum stock level of item in bulk.

so i used DTW template OITM - Items AND OITW - ItemWarehouseInfo template.

But i get the following error.

1

Update FailedBOCM00006 This entry already exists in the following tables (ODBC -2035) This entry already exists in the following tables (ODBC -2035)oItems
2Update FailedBOCM00011 Field cannot be updated (ODBC -1029) Field cannot be updated (ODBC -1029)oItems
3Update FailedBOCM00074 This entry already exists in the following tables (ODBC -2035) This entry already exists in the following tables (ODBC -2035)oItems
     

All item are in Master and used the following template

OITM - Items.csv

ItemCodeMinInventoryDefaultWarehouseLeadTimeToleranceDays
ItemCodeMinLevelDfltWHLeadTimeToleranDay
BOCM0000610CS-III155
BOCM0001130CS-III  15

5

and  ItemWarehouseInfo.csv

ParentKeyLineNumWarehouseCodeMinimalStock
ItemCodeLineNumWhsCodeMinStock
BOCM000110CS-III  30
HSEL000390CS-III50

Please give the solution on it?

entry.title: When I import custom table data using dtw
entry.content:
Why is it that when choosing other Udos in dtw and uploading csv files, there is no error prompt? When choosing this udo and uploading the same csv file, it will prompt ression must evaluate to a node-set.


I chose transacation data and then

entry.title: Delivery Order Draft Query
entry.content:
Hi All

Is it possible to create a Delivery Draft query to show Inventory Item No, Item Description, Quantity and serial number?

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: FMS,SAP B1 HANA
entry.content:
can anyone help me with hana fms query about how to get time difference in hours.I am currently using 12 hour format. time difference is calculated by difference of duration out and duration in.and i want time difference in hours.but some i am not able to get the required output.i am using sap b1 hana version.i have attached my screenshot as well as fms query used.

SELECT
IFNULL(
ROUND(
SECONDS_BETWEEN(
CAST(TO_VARCHAR($[OWOR.U_F_OUT]) AS TIME),
CAST(TO_VARCHAR($[OWOR.U_F_IN]) AS TIME)
) / 3600, 2
),
0)
FROM DUMMY

entry.title: Trial balance report not showing BP filter and GL filter on Selection Criteria screen
entry.content:
Dear 

I have limited finance license.

I am trying to run Trial balance report, which is not showing the BP filter and GL filter on Selection Criteria screen.

Even i select proper date range, it is not given any result but the following error.

"Data is not available; modify selection criteria and re-enter [Message 131-85]"

 

Please what is going wrong ?

entry.title: DTW logs sometimes fails on italian server
entry.content:
Dear Sirs,
we are experiencing an issue with the Data Transfer Workbench (DTW) logs.

When we import documents using DTW, everything appears to work correctly during the session, the DTW interface displays the imported rows and  the errors if any .
However, once we close the DTW application, no entries are saved into the dtw.mdb log file.

We have performed several tests, and the problem appears to be related to the date format or system date. Our system is configured with Italian regional settings (dd/MM/yyyy).

Steps to reproduce:
-Set the PC date to 01 July 2025.
-Set regional settings to Italy (or just configure the date format to dd/MM/yyyy).
-Import any object using DTW (e.g., an Items or a Business Partner).
-Close the DTW interface.
-Open the file located at: %localappdata%\SAP\SAP Business One\Log\Data Transfer Workbench\dtw.mdb

You will notice that:
No records are saved in OLOG or LOG1 tables for the last import.

Note:
If we change the system date to June 30th, 2025 and repeat the same procedure, the log is created correctly.

Could you please confirm if this is a known issue or limitation?
If there is a workaround or a fix available?
Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Marco

entry.title: SAP B1 BY-PRODUCT
entry.content:
Hello,
In my SAP B1 version 9.3, I created a bill of materials (BOM) with a negative quantity declaration for the by-product.
My quantity process is correct and everything works fine. My problem is with the cost of my item; it doesn't include the cost of the by-product.
How can I get the cost of my pure product without the by-product charge?

Any idea?

Regards

#sapb1 #sapbusinessone #production
